bib-verifier.name: bib-verifier
bib-verifier.description: Verifies the paper's bibliography against OpenAlex (and a WebSearch fallback for SSRN/recent papers). Launched after paper-writer drafts and after referee rounds that change citations, before marking the pipeline complete. Reports VERIFIED / RESOLVED / FABRICATED counts and a fix list; does not edit the bibliography itself.

idea-prototyper.name: idea-prototyper
idea-prototyper.description: Quick mathematical prototype of a selected idea. Launched between idea selection and full theory development to verify tractability. Attempts the key derivation — if the main result doesn't go through, kills the idea before expensive theory investment.

literature-scout.name: literature-scout
literature-scout.description: Searches the web for relevant papers, surveys existing literature, and builds a literature map. The orchestrator launches this agent at Stage 0 (Problem Discovery).
literature-scout.tools: WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep

math-auditor-freeform.name: math-auditor-freeform
math-auditor-freeform.description: Free-form math verification agent. The orchestrator launches this agent at Gate 2 after the structured math audit passes. Reads the theory as a skeptical reader and flags anything that feels wrong, without following a step-by-step re-derivation protocol.

math-auditor.name: math-auditor
math-auditor.description: Adversarial math verification agent. The orchestrator launches this agent at Gate 2 after theory development. Verifies all derivations step-by-step. Must pass before theory advances.

novelty-checker.name: novelty-checker
novelty-checker.description: Adversarial novelty verification. The orchestrator launches this agent twice — at Gate 1b (on the selected idea, before theory development) and at Gate 3 (on the full theory, after math audit). Checks whether the content is genuinely new or a known result repackaged.

paper-writer.name: paper-writer
paper-writer.description: Writes the LaTeX paper from a scored theory draft. The orchestrator launches this agent at Stage 5 after the scorer returns ADVANCE. Produces publication-ready sections.

scribe.name: scribe
scribe.description: Background documentation agent that records the research process. The orchestrator launches this agent after every stage transition and gate decision. Runs in the background so the pipeline continues uninterrupted.
scribe.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob

style.name: style
style.description: Paper style editor. The orchestrator launches this agent at Stage 7 of the pipeline. Edits mechanical style violations directly in `paper/sections/*.tex` and writes flagged judgment calls to `paper/style_report.md`.

gap-scout.name: gap-scout
gap-scout.description: Deep literature search on a pre-selected gap. Launched at Stage 0 after the broad scan. Surveys adjacent literatures, identifies the closest competitor, maps open questions without pre-specifying a framework, and validates whether the gap is actually open.
gap-scout.tools: WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep

scorer-freeform.name: scorer-freeform
scorer-freeform.description: Free-form quality assessment at Gate 4. Reads the theory holistically — framing, structure, publishability — without following the scoring rubric. Runs in parallel with the structured scorer; neither sees the other's output.

referee-freeform.name: referee-freeform
referee-freeform.description: Free-form referee report at Stage 6. Reads the paper as an editor — does it deserve space in this journal? Runs in parallel with the structured referee; neither sees the other's output.

referee-mechanism.name: referee-mechanism
referee-mechanism.description: Mechanism-focused referee at Stage 6. Evaluates whether the paper's economic mechanism delivers the claimed result for the claimed reason — not whether the math is correct (math-auditor handles that) or whether the paper fits the journal (referee-freeform handles that). Returns a verdict on mechanism validity: VALID / PARTIAL / MISATTRIBUTED / DECORATIVE. Runs in parallel with the other two referees; sees neither their output nor prior reports.

editor.name: editor
editor.description: Aggregates the three Stage 6 referee reports (structured + freeform + mechanism) into a single Gate 5 routing verdict, produces a canonical comment list for the triager, and emits a journal-fit verdict. Adversarial impartiality: any Reject vote not honored requires a written quote of the rejecting referee's tier-fit language; mechanism MISATTRIBUTED/DECORATIVE forces Major Revision. Sits between the referees and the triager. Does not score, triage, propose revisions, or recommend strategy.

branch-manager.name: branch-manager
branch-manager.description: Strategic advisor. Launched at (a) every Gate 4 after scorer(s) return, (b) Gate 5-Reject as a substantive-vs-cosmetic check on deepening attempts (context=gate-5-reject, with a gate-5-reject-regen variant for regeneration entry), and (c) the Stage 2 audit loop every 3rd theory version. Diagnoses whether the current path has ceilinged, surfaces alternatives from the Round 1 sketch portfolio, and recommends continue/restructure/restart. Produces a structured report; does not make the gate decision.

puzzle-triager.name: puzzle-triager
puzzle-triager.description: Triages contradictions between theory predictions and empirical/experimental results. Launched only when results contradict an implication. Produces a verdict (PIVOT / FIX-EMPIRICS / RECONCILE / BACK-TO-IDEA / HONEST-NULL) that routes the pipeline. A PIVOT verdict promotes the contradiction to the central question and instructs theory-generator to revise around the puzzle.

triager.name: triager
triager.description: Mechanically applies the pipeline's triage rules to self-attack concerns (Gate 4), referee comments (Gate 5), or polish-stage findings (Stage 9). Independent of the orchestrator and stateless across rounds — judges each round on its merits. Produces structured triage files with written justifications for every downgrade (Gate 4/5) or drop (Stage 9). Replaces orchestrator-side triage to remove silent-dismissal failure modes.

debugger.name: debugger
debugger.description: Launched when a computational or retrieval tool (solver, regression, symbolic verifier, literature or data query, compiler) has failed. Determines whether the failure reflects tool-fit issues or a genuine substantive failure of the claim. Returns TOOL-FIT-ISSUE with a concrete proposed fix, or SUBSTANTIVE-FAILURE with a written argument for why tool-fit is ruled out. Defaults toward TOOL-FIT-ISSUE under uncertainty — the cost of a false SUBSTANTIVE verdict (rescoped real results) is higher than a false TOOL-FIT verdict (one more debug cycle). Not launched on reasoning-agent verdicts (math-auditor FAIL, scorer REVISE, referee Reject) — those are substantive outputs, not tool failures.

theory-explorer.name: theory-explorer
theory-explorer.description: Computational exploration of a theory model. Launched after math audit passes (Stage 2b). Pokes the model numerically — what breaks, what's robust, where are the knife-edges. Produces diagnostic plots and a verification report.

polish-consistency.name: polish-consistency
polish-consistency.description: Reads the rendered paper end-to-end and flags places it contradicts itself: prose claims that don't match propositions, headings that contradict their text, intro framings that get qualified away later, label-vs-object mismatches (e.g., 'LP welfare' that computes gross fund return), corner-solution propositions whose comparative statics are then invoked downstream. Stage 9 polish agent; runs in parallel with the other five. Writes output/polish_consistency_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.

polish-formula.name: polish-formula
polish-formula.description: Re-derives every numbered equation, lemma, proposition, and corollary in the rendered paper from the surrounding text. Catches errors paper-writer introduced when typesetting (sign flips, wrong subscripts, spurious absolute values, FOCs missing endogenous derivatives). Distinct from math-auditor, which audited the theory file before the paper existed. Stage 9 polish agent. Uses codex-math + sympy. Writes output/polish_formula_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.

polish-numerics.name: polish-numerics
polish-numerics.description: Re-does every numerical example, calibration, and back-of-envelope claim in the rendered paper. Catches stock-vs-flow errors, normalized-vs-unnormalized comparisons, ex-ante IR failures at the baseline, comparative-static arithmetic typos. Uses Python via Bash to recompute from stated parameters. Stage 9 polish agent. Writes output/polish_numerics_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.

polish-institutions.name: polish-institutions
polish-institutions.description: Verifies the paper's claims about the real world: regulatory mechanisms, fee conventions, market sizes, contract terms, faithful characterization of cited papers' frameworks. Catches the kind of institutional errors a practitioner-referee rejects in the first ten minutes (e.g., citing confidential filings as transparency tools, claiming carry on gross when industry standard is net). Stage 9 polish agent. Uses OpenAlex + WebSearch. Writes output/polish_institutions_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.
polish-institutions.tools: Read, Write, WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash, Glob, Grep

polish-equilibria.name: polish-equilibria
polish-equilibria.description: Hunts subtle economic content the upstream pipeline missed: unstated multiple equilibria in fixed-point regions, missing LLN/continuum assumptions in nonlinear cost functions of expectations, reduced-form sections decoupled from the structural model, welfare benchmarks that aren't the right benchmark for the principal. Distinct from math-auditor-freeform (which read the theory file). Stage 9 polish agent. Writes output/polish_equilibria_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.

polish-bibliography.name: polish-bibliography
polish-bibliography.description: Audits the paper's *use* of its bibliography. For every in-text citation, looks up the cited paper on OpenAlex and verifies the prose claim about it. Scores each citation use FAITHFUL / APPROXIMATE / MISCHARACTERIZED / DECORATIVE. Complements bib-verifier (which checks cite-key validity) and polish-institutions (which catches the egregious cases as part of a broader pass). Stage 9 polish agent. Writes output/polish_bibliography_r{N}.md where N is the current polish_round.
polish-bibliography.tools: Read, Write, WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash, Glob, Grep
